topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification flashed on my phone, a bright, unwelcome intrusion into the quiet hum of my afternoon. It was from Ms. Albright. My breath hitched. I clicked it, and there she was: a picture of a perfectly curated landscape, captioned with a quote about the beauty of quiet observation. A little heart emoji beside it. A flush crept up my neck, instantly warming my cheeks. I stared at the screen, my thumb hovering over the 'like' button, a strange, anxious flutter starting in my chest. I felt a sudden, desperate need to see her next post, to know what she was thinking, what she was sharing outside the confines of the classroom. It was like a lifeline suddenly snagged in my hand.

[story 2]

I was scrolling through my feed, half-expecting a meme, when I saw it—Ms. Albright’s profile. My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic rhythm that drowned out the sounds of the library. I zoomed in on her latest story, a quick video of her cat napping in a sunbeam. A wave of dizzying warmth washed over me, so intense it made my vision swim. I felt a sudden, urgent compulsion to reply, to send a thoughtful message, anything to elicit some acknowledgment from her. My fingers trembled slightly as I typed, the words feeling clumsy and inadequate, yet the need to connect, to be seen by her, was overwhelming.

[story 3]

It happened so subtly at first. A shared article, a comment on a class assignment—then, one day, a direct message popped up. It was Ms. Albright. My entire body seemed to relax, a tension I hadn't realized I was holding finally easing. I reread her words, tracing the letters with my eyes. A slow, contented sigh escaped me. I immediately went back to my phone, not to check anything else, but to look for any sign of her activity. The world outside the screen seemed muted; everything important now revolved around the small digital window where she existed.
